From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Belgian Benedictine monk (1911–1992)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Louis_Leloir "Special:EditPage/Louis Leloir") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Louis Leloir"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Louis+Leloir%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Louis+Leloir%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Louis+Leloir%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Louis+Leloir%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Louis+Leloir%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Louis+Leloir%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2019)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Dom **Louis Leloir**, O.S.B., (1911–1992), originating from [Namur](/wiki/Namur "Namur"), Belgium, was a [Benedictine monk](/wiki/Order_of_Saint_Benedict "Order of Saint Benedict") at the [Abbey of Clervaux](/wiki/Abbey_of_Clervaux "Abbey of Clervaux"), [Clervaux](/wiki/Clervaux "Clervaux"), [Luxembourg](/wiki/Luxembourg "Luxembourg"). His preoccupations were with [oecumenical relations](/wiki/Ecumenism "Ecumenism"), especially with [Judaism](/wiki/Judaism "Judaism"), and with research into early [monasticism](/wiki/Christian_monasticism "Christian monasticism"), [Armenian](/wiki/Armenian_studies "Armenian studies") and [Syriac](/wiki/Syriac_studies "Syriac studies") [patristics](/wiki/Patristics "Patristics") and [apocryphal literature](/wiki/Apocryphal_literature "Apocryphal literature"). Leloir was ordained in 1935,[[1]](#cite_note-1) studied at the [Pontifical Biblical Institute](/wiki/Pontifical_Biblical_Institute "Pontifical Biblical Institute") in Rome, and was also at the Abbey of St. Jerome, Rome. He was granted his doctoral degree (*summa cum laude*) from the [Biblicum](/wiki/Pontifical_Biblical_Institute "Pontifical Biblical Institute") in 1956 for his dissertation on the witness of [Ephrem the Syrian](/wiki/Ephrem_the_Syrian "Ephrem the Syrian") to the [Diatessaron](/wiki/Diatessaron "Diatessaron").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Selected works

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Louis_Leloir&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Selected works")]

* *Commentaire de l'Evangile concordant ou Diatesseron, par Ephrem de Nisibe, traduit du syriaque et de l'arménien*, par Louis Leloir, Cerf, coll. Sources Chrétiennes no. 121, 1966.
* *Le moine selon la tradition arménienne*, Pères Mékhitaristes de Venise, 1969.
* *Désert et Communion - témoignage des Pères du désert recueillis à partir des "Paterica" arméniens*, Bellefontaine, 1978.
* *Ecrits apocryphes sur les Apôtres, traduit de l'édition arménienne de Venise*, Corpus Christianorum: Series Apocryphorum, 2 vol., 1986, 1992.
* "La "lectio divina" chez les Pères du désert", *La Vie Spirituelle*, no. 740, 2001.
* "Les Pères du désert et saint Benoît. Un apophtegme d'Antoine", *Nouvelle Revue Théologique*, no. 102-2, 1980.

A further 22 works by Louis Leloir are listed on Karsruher Virtueller Katalog search engine REGESTA IMPERII published by Akademie Der Wissenschaften unter der Literatur Mainz.
